title: Reverse-order crossed pancake lens with a shaped polarizer
abstract: In various embodiments, a pancake lens block including a shaped reflective polarizer is described. In an embodiment, the shaped reflective polarizer may include an optical element that may be configured to transmit at least a portion of light from a light source. Further, the shaped reflective polarizer may include a wire-grid polarizer that comprises (i) a bolstering substrate, (ii) a wire-grid substrate coupled to the bolstering substrate, and (iii) wire-grids disposed on the wire-grid substrate. The shaped reflective polarizer may be spaced from the optical element by a distance, which may include a cavity filled with a material (such as air or a nanovoided material).
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A pancake lens block, comprising: an optical element configured to transmit at least a portion of radiation from a radiation source; and a shaped reflective polarizer spaced at a distance from the optical element; wherein the shaped reflective polarizer is configured to provide achromatic optical power to the pancake lens block. 2. The pancake lens block of claim 1, wherein the optical element comprises: a first retarder having a first axis of orientation, where the first retarder is configured to selectively transmit a portion of the radiation from the radiation source; a partial reflector that receives the portion of radiation from the first retarder and transmits the portion of radiation; and a second retarder having a surface at a distance from the shaped reflective polarizer, where the second retarder is oriented to a second axis of orientation that is substantially orthogonal to the first axis of orientation, and where the second retarder transmits the portion of radiation from the partial reflector. 3. The pancake lens block of claim 2, wherein the shaped reflective polarizer is configured to reflect the portion of radiation transmitted by the second retarder back to the partial reflector through the second retarder, where the partial reflector reflects a second portion of the radiation from the second retarder back to the shaped reflective polarizer. 4. The pancake lens block of claim 2, wherein the shaped reflective polarizer comprises a wire-grid polarizer that comprises: (i) a bolstering substrate, (ii) a wire-grid substrate coupled to the bolstering substrate, and (iii) wire-grids disposed on the wire-grid substrate. 5. The pancake lens block of claim 4, wherein the bolstering substrate provides mechanical support to the wire-grid polarizer and maintains the polarization state of the portion of radiation transmitted by the second retarder. 6. The pancake lens block of claim 4, wherein the bolstering substrate comprises a tri-acetyl cellulose (TAC) material or a poly-methyl methacrylate (PMMA) material. 7. The pancake lens block of claim 4, wherein the wire-grid polarizer is disposed on a lens having a radius of curvature, and the thickness of the bolstering substrate is at least partially based on the radius of curvature. 8. The pancake lens block of claim 4, wherein the wire-grid polarizer is thermoformed and tire bolstering substrate has a glass transition temperature characterized by an associated rate of change with temperature being below a threshold. 9. The pancake lens block of claim 4, wherein the wire-grid substrate comprises a TAC material or a PMMA material. The pancake lens block of claim 4, wherein the wire-grids comprise a TAC material. 11. The pancake lens block of claim 1, wherein the shaped reflective polarizer has a surface that is convex, concave, flat, or irregular. 12. The pancake lens block of claim 1 , wherein: the distance is configured to provide additional optical path length in the pancake lens block; and the distance is provided by at least one of an air gap or a material. 13. A head mounted display (HMD) comprising: an electronic display configured to emit light; and a pancake lens block comprising: an optical element configured to transmit at least a portion of the light emitted by the electronic display; and a shaped reflective polarizer spaced at a distance from the optical element; wherein the shaped reflective polarizer is configured to provide achromatic optical power to the pancake lens block. 14. The HMD of claim 13, wherein the optical element comprises: a first retarder having a first axis of orientation, where the first retarder is configured to selectively transmit a portion of the light emitted by the electronic display; a partial reflector that receives tire portion of light from the first retarder and transmits the portion of light; and a second retarder having a surface at a distance from the shaped reflective polarizer, and where the second retarder is oriented to a second axis of orientation that is substantially orthogonal to the first axis of orientation, and where the second retarder transmits the portion of light from the partial reflector. 15. The HMD of claim 14, wherein the shaped reflective polarizer is configured to reflect the portion of light transmitted by the second retarder back to the partial reflector through the second retarder, where the partial reflector reflects a second portion of the light from the second retarder back to the shaped reflective polarizer. 16. The HMD of claim 13, wherein the shaped reflective polarizer comprises a wire-grid polarizer that comprises (i) a bolstering substrate, (ii) a wire-grid substrate coupled to die bolstering substrate, and (iii) wire-grids disposed on the wire-grid substrate. 17. The HMD of claim 16, wherein the bolstering substrate comprises a tri-acetyl cellulose (TAC) material or a poly-methyl methacrylate (PMMA) material. 18. The HMD of claim 16, wherein the wire-grid polarizer is disposed on a lens having a radius of curvature, and the thickness of the bolstering substrate is at least partially based on the radius of curvature. 19. A method of assembling a pancake lens block, the method comprising: configurating an optical element to transmit at least a portion of radiation from a radiation source: and spacing a shaped reflective polarizer from the optical element by a distance; wherein the shaped reflective polarizer is configured to provide achromatic optical power to the pancake lens block. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the shaped reflective polarizer comprises a wire-grid polarizer that comprises (i) a bolstering substrate, (ii) a wire-grid substrate coupled to the bolstering substrate, and (iii) wire-grids disposed on the wire-grid substrate.
